description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a large, convex glass screen encased in a thick, cream-colored plastic bezel. The front features a black frame around the screen and a small control panel with colored indicator lights and a 'RETRO TV' label. The sides and top are finished in a woodgrain veneer, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. One side has a rectangular vent and a large speaker grille, while the other side features two rotary dials for channel and volume control. The back is black plastic with ventilation slats and recessed panels, likely for access to internal components. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of analog televisions from the 1970s or 1980s.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a convex glass screen, woodgrain sides, and rotary dials, evoking a classic mid-century style.
description_view_0: The front view shows the convex glass screen, thick cream bezel, black frame, and a small control panel with indicator lights and a 'RETRO TV' label.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the woodgrain finish, a rectangular vent, and a large black speaker grille.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a black plastic back with ventilation slats and recessed access panels.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the woodgrain finish and two large rotary dials for channel and volume control.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, cream-colored plastic bezel with a black inner frame and a small control panel at the bottom right.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two rotary dials on the right side panel; small buttons on the front lower right.
